# Weekly Blog Upload Guide

This guide walks through the weekly process of uploading new blog posts to **Mókshara** (`https://mokshara.indevs.in`).

There are two ways to upload posts:
* **Method 1: Using the Automated Python Script** (Fastest & prevents errors)
* **Method 2: Manually through the GitHub Website** (No tools required)

---

## Folder Structure Reference

* `/blog/` — Contains all blog post HTML files (e.g. `caregiver-burnout-signs.html`).
* `/blog/blog_post_template.html` — The master design template. Do not edit this file directly.
* `/blog/index.html` — The main Blog Landing Page listing all articles.
* `/assets/` — Contains blog images (placed at the root directory level).
* `/sitemap.xml` — Tells search engines about new pages.
* `add_new_blog.py` — The automated command-line tool.

---

## Method 1: The Automated Python Script (Recommended)

This script prompts you for details, generates the article HTML from the template, updates the blog landing page grid, and appends the post to `sitemap.xml` automatically.

### Prerequisites:
* Python 3 installed on your computer.

### Steps:
1. Place any new article images in the root `/assets/` folder.
2. Open **Terminal** (Mac/Linux) or **Command Prompt** (Windows) in your local `mokshara-store` repository folder.
3. Run the script:
   ```bash
   python add_new_blog.py
   ```
4. Follow the interactive questions:
   * **Blog Title**: Enter the heading (e.g. `Healing Trauma Through Somatics`).
   * **Slug**: Press Enter to auto-generate a clean URL slug (e.g. `healing-trauma-through-somatics`).
   * **Subtitle/Hook**: Write a short engaging sentence.
   * **Category**: Type the badge label (e.g. `Somatic Healing` or `Eldercare`).
   * **Author**: Type the author's name (e.g. `Devi` or `Jyothi Talari`).
   * **Read Time**: (e.g. `4 min read`).
   * **Meta Description**: A 150-character summary for Google search listings.
   * **Hero Image**: Type the name of the image you placed in `/assets/` (e.g. `hero-somatics.jpeg`).
   * **Content**: Paste or type the article paragraphs. You can also type the path of a text file (e.g. `drafts/post1.txt`) to load the content.
     * *Note: Double Enter starts a new paragraph. Write `## Section Header` for large headers.*
     * *When typing manually, type `EOF` on a new line and press Enter to finish.*
   * **FAQs**: Choose `y` to interactively add Q&A accordions.
5. Review the files the script modified for you:
   * A new post was created at `blog/[slug].html` and `2026/07/[slug].html`
   * Card added to `blog/index.html`
   * URL entry added to `sitemap.xml`
6. Push the changes live to GitHub Pages:
   ```bash
   git add .
   git commit -m "Publish blog: [Title]"
   git push origin main
   ```

---

## Method 2: Manual Upload on GitHub Website (No Installation Option)

If you do not have Git or Python installed, you can upload pages manually:

1. **Upload Images**:
   * Open [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/Talarijyothi48/mokshara-store).
   * Click the **assets** folder.
   * Click **Add file** -> **Upload files**, drag your hero/article images, and commit changes.

2. **Create the Article File**:
   * Copy the template content from [blog/blog_post_template.html](blog/blog_post_template.html).
   * Navigate to the **blog** folder.
   * Click **Add file** -> **Create new file**.
   * Name it `your-article-slug.html` (use lowercase and hyphens).
   * Paste the template code.
   * Replace all capitalized placeholders (like `{{TITLE}}`, `{{HERO_IMAGE}}`, `{{CONTENT}}`) with your article details and HTML paragraphs.
   * Click **Commit changes** to save.

3. **Add the Card to the Blog Page**:
   * Open [blog/index.html](blog/index.html) and click the pencil icon to edit.
   * Find `<div class="blog-grid">`.
   * Add a new article card structure below the marker:
     ```html
     <!-- Article: Title -->
     <article class="blog-card">
       <div class="blog-card-image">
         <img src="/assets/your-image.jpeg" alt="Article Title">
         <span class="blog-card-badge">Category</span>
       </div>
       <div class="blog-card-body">
         <div class="blog-card-meta">✍️ By Author · 3 min read</div>
         <h3 class="blog-card-title">Article Title</h3>
         <p class="blog-card-desc">
           Short article snippet description...
         </p>
         <a href="/blog/your-article-slug.html" class="blog-card-link">Read Article ➔</a>
       </div>
     </article>
     ```
   * Click **Commit changes**.

4. **Update the Sitemap**:
   * Open [sitemap.xml](sitemap.xml) and click edit.
   * Before the closing `</urlset>` tag, insert:
     ```xml
     <url>
       <loc>https://mokshara.indevs.in/blog/your-article-slug.html</loc>
       <lastmod>YYYY-MM-DD</lastmod>
       <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
       <priority>0.8</priority>
       <image:image>
         <image:loc>https://mokshara.indevs.in/assets/your-image.jpeg</image:loc>
         <image:title>Article Title</image:title>
         <image:caption>Short article snippet description...</image:caption>
       </image:image>
     </url>
     ```
   * Click **Commit changes**.

Once committed, GitHub Pages will automatically compile and deploy your new post within 1–2 minutes!
